body: PR description lists new coverage for methods like MilesToKilometers/KilometersToMiles, DegreesToRadians/RadiansToDegrees, PoundsToKilograms/KilogramsToPounds, and AtmospheresToPascals, but those tests already exist earlier in this file and are not part of this diff. Meanwhile the new tests added here cover different methods (e.g., Fahrenheit/Celsius, degrees-per-second↔hertz, kPa/hPa→pascals). Please update the PR description (and possibly title) so it accurately reflects the actual tests added in this change.

body:
🟢 **MINOR** (2/3 reviewers)

The existing `MilesToKilometers` test includes a negative case (`[InlineData(-1, -1.6093)]`) and a sub-unit fractional case. For consistency, consider adding a negative input here:

```csharp
[InlineData(-1, -1609.344)]
```

The converter is a simple multiplication, so this is unlikely to catch a bug, but it keeps the coverage style consistent across sibling converter tests.

🟢 **MINOR** (2/3 reviewers)

`RadiansPerSecondToHertz` has only 3 test cases (including zero) while other new tests have 4–5. Consider adding a multi-revolution case for parity:

```csharp
[InlineData(12.5664, 2.0000)]
```

More broadly, the new frequency conversion tests (`DegreesPerSecondToHertz`, `RadiansPerSecondToHertz`, `HertzToDegreesPerSecond`, `HertzToRadiansPerSecond`) only cover zero/positive values. Existing converter tests in this file often include a negative input. Consider adding one negative case per frequency test for sign-handling coverage consistency.
